title: extract_response coupled to build_request via hardcoded call_id "r1"
description: Every method in methods.rs calls build_request(...) which hardcodes "r1" as the call ID, then calls extract_response(&resp, "r1"). The two halves are coupled through a string constant that neither side owns. If build_request is ever changed or extended to produce multiple invocations, the string must be updated in both places by the caller -- the compiler will not catch the mismatch. Rewrite: have build_request return (JmapRequest, &'static str) where the second element is the call ID it used, and thread that returned value into extract_response. Or: since every request here has exactly one invocation, make extract_response work on the first method_response directly without requiring a call_id lookup. Either way, remove the invisible coupling.
close_reason: Addressed: 4u4.15 added invariant comment documenting the r1 constraint
